title: GPTs are GPTs: An Early Look at the Labor Market Impact Potential of Large Language Models
authors: Eloundou Tyna; Manning Sam; Mishkin Pamela; Rock Daniel
relevance_note: Seminal paper estimating ~80% of US workforce has >=10% tasks exposed to LLMs; foundational for AI-job displacement research.

title: Generate the Future of Work: Evidence from an Online Labor Market
authors: Demirci Ozge; Hannane Jonas; Squicciarini Mariagrazia
relevance_note: Studies how generative AI affects online labor markets; finds reduced demand for writing/coding freelancers.

title: AI-Exposed Jobs Deteriorated Before ChatGPT
authors: Kogan Leonid; Papanikolaou Dimitris; Schmidt Lawrence; Seegmiller Bryan
relevance_note: Shows AI-exposed occupations already showed wage/employment declines before ChatGPT; important for causal claims.

title: Strategic Responses to Technological Change
relevance_note: Examines how freelancers strategically adapt to AI tools—upskilling, niche specialization, or exit.

title: Beyond Automation: Rethinking Work Creativity and Governance in the Age of AI
relevance_note: Argues AI governance must go beyond automation framing; discusses creativity, worker agency, and policy design.

title: Mapping the Regulatory Learning Space for the EU AI Act
relevance_note: Analyzes the EU AI Act's regulatory learning space; implications for AI workforce regulation.

title: The EU AI Audit Ecosystem Beyond the AI Act
relevance_note: Examines gaps in EU AI Act audit ecosystem; relevant to enforcement of labor-protecting AI regulation.

title: AI and the Macroeconomy: Expert Disagreements and New Results
relevance_note: Surveys expert disagreements on AI's macroeconomic impact—productivity gains vs. inequality and job loss.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Society: A Survey
relevance_note: Broad survey of AI's societal impact including labor displacement, bias, and governance challenges.

title: AI and Freelance Labor Markets
authors: Hui Xiang; Reshef Olga; Zhou Luofeng
relevance_note: Studies ChatGPT's impact on freelance platforms; finds significant earnings decline for writing/coding jobs.

title: AI and Technological Unemployment: A Systematic Review
relevance_note: Systematic review of AI-driven technological unemployment; synthesizes evidence across sectors.

title: Reforming the Tax Code for an AI-Driven Workforce
relevance_note: Proposes tax code reforms to support workers displaced by AI; discusses UBI-adjacent fiscal mechanisms.

title: AI Will Transform the Global Economy: Let's Make Sure It Benefits Humanity
relevance_note: IMF analysis: AI will affect ~40% of global jobs; advanced economies more exposed; calls for safety nets.

title: Universal Basic Income in the Age of Automation
relevance_note: Examines UBI as a response to automation-driven job displacement; feasibility and design considerations.

title: Automation Generative AI and Job Displacement Risk
relevance_note: SHRM data brief on job displacement risk from automation and generative AI; practitioner perspective.
